_comment: DO NOT EDIT: File auto generated by Pelican Export System. https://pelican.dev
name: Hoodik
author: quentin.claude@godefroid.eu
description: Hoodik is a lightweight, secure, self-hosted cloud storage. Built on end-to-end encryption, all uploads and file metadata are encrypted in the browser before being sent to the server, so the server never sees your data in plaintext.

installation.script:
#!/bin/bash
# Hoodik runs entirely from the official Docker image.
# No installation steps needed; the container entrypoint handles everything.
echo "Hoodik egg — nothing to install. Container image: hudikhq/hoodik:latest"

variables.name: App URL
variables.description: Publicly accessible URL for this Hoodik instance (e.g. https://files.example.com). Used to build share links. Do not add a trailing slash.

variables.name: HTTP Port
variables.description: Port Hoodik listens on inside the container. Must match the allocation port assigned to the server.

variables.name: Disable SSL (recommended for Pelican)
variables.description: Set to 'true' to let Hoodik serve plain HTTP. Keep true when Pelican or a reverse proxy terminates TLS.

variables.name: Data Directory
variables.description: Path inside the container where uploaded files and the database are stored. Map this to a persistent volume.

variables.name: Master Key Secret
variables.description: Random secret used to derive the server-side encryption key. Generate with: openssl rand -hex 32. Changing this after first run will make all existing data unreadable.

variables.name: Admin Email
variables.description: Email address of the initial administrator account created on first launch.

variables.name: Admin Password
variables.description: Password for the initial administrator account. Change immediately after first login.

variables.name: Max Upload Size (MB)
variables.description: Maximum size in megabytes for a single file upload. Default is 10240 (10 GB).

variables.name: Registration Enabled
variables.description: Allow new users to register. Set to 'false' to restrict the instance to invited or manually created accounts.

variables.name: SMTP Host
variables.description: (Optional) SMTP server hostname for outgoing email (invitations, password reset). Leave blank to disable email.

variables.name: From Email Address
variables.description: (Optional) The 'From' address used in outgoing emails.

variables.name: Log Level
variables.description: Logging verbosity. One of: error, warn, info, debug, trace.
